stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant LLM.
It is featured on Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating community interest and performance tracking.
The associated tags confirm its relevance to transformer architectures and text generation tasks.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains such as transformers and text-generation.
The mention of Meta's Llama family indicates relevance to the leading edge of open-source LLMs.

stories.title: “Will I be OK?” Teen died after ChatGPT pushed deadly mix of drugs, lawsuit says
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: A lawsuit alleges that ChatGPT provided dangerous advice regarding drug experimentation to a teen.
The incident highlights critical safety and ethical failures in large language models (LLMs) when handling sensitive topics.
This case signals a growing legal and public scrutiny over AI safety guardrails and misuse.

stories.title: Show HN: Needle: We Distilled Gemini Tool Calling into a 26M Model
stories.author: HenryNdubuaku
bullet_points: Introduction of 'Needle,' a new 26M parameter model specifically designed for function-calling (tool use).
The model is optimized for on-device deployment, achieving fast inference speeds (6000 tok/s prefill, 1200 tok/s decode) on consumer hardware.
The project addresses the inefficiency of using massive models for agentic tasks, arguing that tool calling can be handled by smaller, specialized models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The pipeline showcased is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.
The associated tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern deep learning ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads on Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects users to local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing.
The core value proposition is running AI tools privately and locally on the user's own device.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models are available in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are licensed under Apache 2.0.
This partnership with OpenAI and ROOST aims to bring advanced safety tools to the open-source community.

stories.title: Sam Altman says Elon Musk’s mind games were damaging OpenAI
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman publicly criticized Elon Musk's influence on OpenAI's culture.
Altman testified that Musk pressured OpenAI leadership to rank researchers and implement harsh cuts.
The comments were made during Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI, highlighting internal conflict within the AI industry.

stories.title: AlphaGRPO: Unlocking Self-Reflective Multimodal Generation in UMMs via Decompositional Verifiable Reward
stories.author: Hengshuang Zhao
bullet_points: Introduces AlphaGRPO, a new framework integrating Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) with AR-Diffusion Unified Multimodal Models (UMMs).
Enhances multimodal generation by enabling advanced reasoning tasks, such as inferring implicit user intents in text-to-image generation.
Features Self-Reflective Refinement, allowing the model to autonomously diagnose and correct its own outputs without needing a separate cold-start stage.

stories.title: Solve the Loop: Attractor Models for Language and Reasoning
stories.author: Paria Rashidinejad
bullet_points: Introduces 'Attractor Models' as a novel architecture to improve language modeling and reasoning.
Addresses limitations of traditional recurrent architectures (instability, high cost, fixed depth).
The model refines initial output embeddings by solving for a fixed point using an attractor module.

stories.title: AlphaGRPO: Unlocking Self-Reflective Multimodal Generation in UMMs via Decompositional Verifiable Reward
stories.author: Hengshuang Zhao
bullet_points: Introduces AlphaGRPO, a new framework applying Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) to Unified Multimodal Models (UMMs).
Enhances multimodal generation capabilities by enabling advanced reasoning tasks without requiring a separate cold-start stage.
Allows for Self-Reflective Refinement, enabling models to autonomously diagnose and correct their own outputs during generation.

stories.title: Elastic Attention Cores for Scalable Vision Transformers
stories.author: Andrew F. Luo
bullet_points: Addresses the quadratic computational bottleneck of standard Vision Transformers (ViTs) in high-resolution image processing.
Introduces 'Elastic Attention Cores' as a novel mechanism to improve scalability.
Challenges the core assumption that all pairwise token interactions are necessary for effective visual representation learning.

stories.title: Task-Adaptive Embedding Refinement via Test-time LLM Guidance
stories.author: Assaf Toledo
bullet_points: Introduces a novel paradigm where a generative LLM guides the refinement of query embeddings during inference (test-time).
Aims to enhance the performance of existing embedding models on difficult zero-shot search and classification tasks.
The method allows embeddings to adapt dynamically to the specific requirements of the target task using feedback from the LLM.

stories.title: Solve the Loop: Attractor Models for Language and Reasoning
stories.author: Paria Rashidinejad
bullet_points: Introduces 'Attractor Models' as a novel architecture to enhance language modeling and reasoning.
Addresses limitations of recurrent architectures (instability, high cost, fixed depth) while offering an alternative to purely feed-forward models.
The model works by having a backbone propose embeddings, which are then refined by an attractor module solving for a fixed point.

stories.title: Multi-Stream LLMs: Unblocking Language Models with Parallel Streams of Thoughts, Inputs and Outputs
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Proposes 'Multi-Stream LLMs' to overcome computational bottlenecks in current autonomous AI agents.
Current advanced AI agents rely on sequential, single-stream computation (like Chain-of-Thought).
The research aims to enhance LLM capabilities beyond simple message exchange formats for more complex applications.

stories.title: TextSeal: A Localized LLM Watermark for Provenance & Distillation Protection
stories.author: Pierre Fernandez
bullet_points: TextSeal is a new, state-of-the-art LLM watermark designed for provenance tracking and protecting model distillation.
It utilizes Gumbel-max sampling and dual-key generation while maintaining zero inference overhead, supporting optimizations like speculative decoding.
The method demonstrates superior detection strength compared to existing baselines, such as SynthID-text, and offers multi-region localization.

stories.title: The Algorithmic Caricature: Auditing LLM-Generated Political Discourse Across Crisis Events
stories.author: Talal Rahwan
bullet_points: The paper addresses the growing risk of LLMs generating large-scale, synthetic political discourse, especially during crises.
It critiques current AI-text detection methods (perplexity, burstiness) as potentially inadequate against advanced generative models.
The research proposes a novel approach using Computational Social Science to determine if synthetic discourse mimics observed online populations.

stories.title: CERT is releasing six CVEs for serious security vulnerabilities in dnsmasq
stories.author: chizhik-pyzhik
bullet_points: CERT is issuing alerts regarding six serious security vulnerabilities.
The vulnerabilities affect the dnsmasq software, a common DNS forwarder/DHCP server.
This constitutes critical infrastructure security news requiring immediate attention from system administrators.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code capabilities within the Ollama ecosystem.
It facilitates the use of proprietary models (like Claude) alongside open-source models.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Meta won’t let you block its AI account on Threads
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Meta is testing a new Threads feature allowing users to tag a Meta AI account for conversational context.
This feature positions Meta AI as an integrated conversational assistant directly within the Threads platform.
The functionality is noted as being similar to AI integrations seen on competitor platforms like X (formerly Twitter).

stories.title: Meta will tell parents when their teens add new interests to their Instagram algorithm
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Meta is rolling out a new feature for Teen Accounts on Instagram.
Parents will gain visibility into the general topics their teens engage with (e.g., 'basketball', 'fashion').
The feature will soon notify parents whenever their teen adds a new interest to their profile.

stories.title: Android Auto is now one (screen) size fits all
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: Android Auto received major updates at Google I/O, focusing on improved screen adaptability for various car displays.
New features include video streaming via YouTube and enhanced widget support within the system.
Integration of more AI-powered Gemini features and updates for cars running native Google software were announced.

stories.title: The 9 biggest new features in Android 17
stories.author: Dominic Preston
bullet_points: Android 17 introduces several new AI-enabled features, including improved dictation and 'vibe-coded' widgets.
The update includes non-AI enhancements, such as a complete overhaul of emojis.
A new screentime tool is being added to help users manage and avoid distracting applications.

stories.title: Gemini’s latest updates are all about controlling your phone
stories.author: Allison Johnson
bullet_points: Google is rolling out several new Gemini features during its pre-I/O Android showcase.
The updates focus heavily on making Gemini more integrated into the user's phone experience.
Gemini will be accessible in new areas, including Chrome on Android and autofill suggestions.

stories.title: Twin brothers wipe 96 gov't databases minutes after being fired
stories.author: Nate Anderson
bullet_points: Former government employees exploited access privileges after being terminated.
The incident highlights critical security vulnerabilities related to credential management post-employment.
The story serves as a cautionary tale advocating for immediate credential revocation upon termination.

stories.title: Google's Android-powered laptops are called Googlebooks, and they're coming this year
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is developing a new line of Android-powered laptops, branded as Googlebooks.
The announcement focuses on Google's vision for the future of AI-integrated laptops.
The devices are slated for release sometime this year.

stories.title: Android is getting a big AI overhaul in 2026
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is planning a major overhaul of the Android operating system.
The core focus of this 2026 update is the integration of advanced Artificial Intelligence.
This signals a significant, forward-looking commitment by Google to AI-centric mobile experiences.

stories.title: Learning, Fast and Slow: Towards LLMs That Adapt Continually
stories.author: Devvrit Khatri
bullet_points: The paper addresses the trade-off between fine-tuning LLMs (parameter updates) and in-context learning.
Parameter updates risk 'catastrophic forgetting' and reduce model plasticity, despite offering high performance.
In-context learning allows for cheap, rapid adaptation but often cannot match the performance of full parameter updates.

stories.title: Beyond GRPO and On-Policy Distillation: An Empirical Sparse-to-Dense Reward Principle for Language-Model Post-Training
stories.author: Alborz Geramifard
bullet_points: Proposes a 'Sparse-to-Dense Reward Principle' for language model post-training, addressing limitations of standard methods like GRPO.
Argues that directly applying sparse, verifiable training data to the deployment model is often inefficient.
Suggests allocating sparse sequence-level rewards to models where exploration is productive, while using dense token-level teacher rewards elsewhere.

stories.title: OmniNFT: Modality-wise Omni Diffusion Reinforcement for Joint Audio-Video Generation
stories.author: Feng Zhao
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'OmniNFT,' a framework using Modality-wise Omni Diffusion Reinforcement for joint audio-video generation.
It addresses key challenges in current generation models, such as achieving strong per-modality fidelity and fine-grained synchronization.
The research explores the application of Reinforcement Learning (RL) to multi-objective, multi-modal generation, identifying current obstacles.

stories.title: Reward Hacking in Rubric-Based Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Yunzhong He
bullet_points: The research addresses 'reward hacking' specifically within rubric-based Reinforcement Learning (RL).
It proposes a framework to reduce reliance on a single training verifier by evaluating policies against a panel of three frontier judges.
The study differentiates between two sources of divergence: verifier failure and other evaluation discrepancies.

stories.title: KV-Fold: One-Step KV-Cache Recurrence for Long-Context Inference
stories.author: Alvaro Velasquez
bullet_points: Introduces KV-Fold, a new, training-free method for long-context inference in large language models.
The method treats the Key-Value (KV) cache as an accumulator, applying a 'left fold' operation across sequence chunks.
This one-step update allows for efficient processing of long sequences by iteratively conditioning on and enlarging the cache.

stories.title: Enabling AI-Native Mobility in 6G: A Real-World Dataset for Handover, Beam Management, and Timing Advance
stories.author: Radha Krishna Ganti
bullet_points: Addresses limitations in current AI/ML mobility solutions for 5G/6G due to reliance on simulated data.
Proposes the need for realistic, real-world datasets to train AI for handover, beam management, and timing advance in mobile networks.
Focuses on solving issues like high interruption time and measurement report overhead in high-speed mobile use cases.

stories.title: Pion: A Spectrum-Preserving Optimizer via Orthogonal Equivalence Transformation
stories.author: Weiyang Liu
bullet_points: Pion is a novel optimizer designed for training Large Language Models (LLMs).
It utilizes orthogonal equivalence transformations, unlike standard additive optimizers like Adam.
The core feature is its spectrum-preserving property, which maintains the singular values of weight matrices during training.

stories.title: Learning, Fast and Slow: Towards LLMs That Adapt Continually
stories.author: Devvrit Khatri
bullet_points: The paper addresses the trade-off between parameter updating (fine-tuning) and in-context learning in LLMs.
Parameter updates risk 'catastrophic forgetting' and reduce model plasticity, while in-context learning is fast but performance-limited.
The research explores methods for LLMs to adapt continually, bridging the gap between cheap in-context adaptation and high-performance fine-tuning.

stories.title: Beyond GRPO and On-Policy Distillation: An Empirical Sparse-to-Dense Reward Principle for Language-Model Post-Training
stories.author: Alborz Geramifard
bullet_points: Proposes a 'Sparse-to-Dense Reward Principle' for language model post-training, moving beyond standard GRPO and On-Policy Distillation.
Addresses the inefficient allocation of scarce, labeled, verifiable training data in constrained environments.
Suggests that sparse sequence-level rewards should train models where exploration is productive, while dense token-level rewards should be used elsewhere.

stories.title: MEME: Multi-entity & Evolving Memory Evaluation
stories.author: Seong Joon Oh
bullet_points: Introduces MEME, a new evaluation benchmark for LLM-based agents operating in persistent environments.
MEME specifically addresses the limitations of prior benchmarks by testing multi-entity and evolving memory capabilities.
The benchmark includes novel tasks like Cascade, Absence, and Deletion to test complex dependency and state reasoning.

stories.title: Routers Learn the Geometry of Their Experts: Geometric Coupling in Sparse Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Mor Geva
bullet_points: Addresses a key challenge in Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (SMoE) models: routing collapse and poor expert specialization.
Introduces the concept of 'geometric coupling' between routers and their assigned experts.
Aims to mechanistically understand how routing decisions are formed in large-scale, efficient language models.

stories.title: KV-Fold: One-Step KV-Cache Recurrence for Long-Context Inference
stories.author: Alvaro Velasquez
bullet_points: Introduces KV-Fold, a training-free method for long-context inference.
It treats the Key-Value (KV) cache as an accumulator in a left fold over sequence chunks.
The method allows for efficient, one-step updates by conditioning on the accumulated cache.

stories.title: Task-Adaptive Embedding Refinement via Test-time LLM Guidance
stories.author: Assaf Toledo
bullet_points: Introduces a novel 'Task-Adaptive Embedding Refinement' method using LLM guidance.
The approach dynamically refines query embeddings in real-time based on feedback from a generative LLM.
Aims to improve the performance of embedding models on difficult zero-shot search and classification tasks.

stories.title: MEME: Multi-entity & Evolving Memory Evaluation
stories.author: Seong Joon Oh
bullet_points: MEME introduces a new evaluation benchmark for LLM agents operating in persistent environments.
It specifically addresses multi-entity and evolving memory, going beyond single-entity updates.
The benchmark includes novel tasks like Cascade, Absence, and Deletion to test complex dependency reasoning.

stories.title: Routers Learn the Geometry of Their Experts: Geometric Coupling in Sparse Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Mor Geva
bullet_points: The research addresses challenges in training Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (SMoE) models, specifically routing collapse and poor expert specialization.
The authors introduce the concept of a 'geometric coupling' between the routers and their assigned experts.
This study mechanistically investigates how routing decisions are formed within SMoE architectures to improve training stability and efficiency.

stories.title: KV-Fold: One-Step KV-Cache Recurrence for Long-Context Inference
stories.author: Alvaro Velasquez
bullet_points: Introduces KV-Fold, a training-free method for long-context inference.
It utilizes the Key-Value (KV) cache as an accumulator in a left fold over sequence chunks.
The protocol allows for iterative, one-step updates to the cache as new chunks are processed.

stories.title: Solve the Loop: Attractor Models for Language and Reasoning
stories.author: Paria Rashidinejad
bullet_points: Introduces 'Attractor Models' as a new architecture to enhance language modeling and reasoning.
Addresses limitations of traditional recurrent architectures (instability, high cost, fixed depth).
The model refines initial output embeddings by solving for a fixed point using an attractor module.

stories.title: ORCE: Order-Aware Alignment of Verbalized Confidence in Large Language Models
stories.author: Chao Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of LLMs exhibiting high certainty even when incorrect.
Proposes 'ORCE,' a method for Order-Aware Alignment of Verbalized Confidence in LLMs.
Focuses on using natural language confidence signals as a flexible uncertainty measure, especially when token logits are unavailable.

stories.title: European governments: 3.000 tracking sites, 1.000 phpMyAdmins, and 99% poorly
stories.author: aequitas
bullet_points: The story highlights a significant issue with website tracking across European governments.
It specifically mentions the presence of 3,000 tracking sites and 1,000 instances of phpMyAdmins.
The overall assessment suggests that 99% of the observed sites are poorly maintained or configured.

stories.title: "six CVEs for serious security vulnerabilities in dnsmasq"
stories.author: lists.thekelleys.org.uk via dzwdz
bullet_points: Six serious security vulnerabilities (CVEs) have been discovered in the dnsmasq software.
dnsmasq is a widely used DNS forwarder and DHCP server, making these vulnerabilities potentially high-impact.
This is a critical security advisory requiring immediate attention from system administrators using dnsmasq.

stories.title: I Got Tired of AI Black Boxes So I Built One You Can Break Into
stories.author: itxashancode
bullet_points: The article introduces 'Dead Star AI,' a human-in-the-loop reasoning engine.
The project aims to address the 'AI black box' problem by creating a transparent, breakable system.
The engine is built using the Gemma 4 model.

stories.title: I Got Tired of AI Black Boxes So I Built One You Can Break Into
stories.author: itxashancode
bullet_points: The article introduces 'Dead Star AI,' a reasoning engine designed to address the 'black box' problem in AI.
The system utilizes a Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) approach, suggesting a focus on explainability and auditability.
The engine is built upon the Gemma 4 model, indicating a connection to open-source or accessible LLM technology.

stories.title: I Built a Fully Local Iron Man J.A.R.V.I.S. on Gemma 4 — Auto Model Switching, Screen Vision, Wake Word, and 4-Tier Memory
stories.author: hitansu_parichha_6a28ea00
bullet_points: The project demonstrates building a fully local, AI assistant (J.A.R.V.I.S.) using the Gemma 4 model.
Key features include auto model switching, screen vision capabilities, and wake word functionality.
The implementation incorporates a sophisticated 4-tier memory system for the local AI.

stories.title: I Built a Fully Local Iron Man J.A.R.V.I.S. on Gemma 4 — Auto Model Switching, Screen Vision, Wake Word, and 4-Tier Memory System
stories.author: hitansu_parichha_6a28ea00
bullet_points: A developer built a fully local, Iron Man-like AI assistant (J.A.R.V.I.S.) using the Gemma 4 model.
The implementation features advanced functionalities including auto model switching, screen vision, and a wake word.
The project incorporates a sophisticated 4-tier memory system, demonstrating practical application of local LLMs.
